locations.name: Chaîne de la Selle
locations.summary: Chaîne de la Selle (French pronunciation: [ʃɛn də la sɛl]) is a mountain range in Haiti, on the island of Hispaniola.

locations.name: Massif de la Hotte
locations.summary: The Massif de la Hotte (French pronunciation: [masif də la ɔt]) is a mountain range in southwestern Haiti, on the Tibur…

locations.name: 次生林
locations.summary: 次生林，又名天然次生林、再生林、再生森林、二次林。是指原始穩定的森林植被遭到人類活動干擾（刀耕火種、林木砍伐）或同等破壞性的自然現象之後，主要透過自然過程再生形成的森林或林地區域。其特徵有別於未曾遭受近期干擾的原始森林、複雜的早期遷移階段…

locations.name: 聖多明哥
locations.summary: 圣多明各（西班牙語：Santo Domingo，发音：[ˈsanto ðoˈmiŋɡo]），全名聖多明各德古斯曼（Santo Domingo de Guzmán），位於多米尼加共和國的國家特區內，為多米尼加的首都。它是多米尼加共和國的首都…

relations.subject: Hispaniolan parakeet
relations.evidence: It is very rare in Haiti, where in the early 2000s it was found in the Chaîne de la Selle and Massif de la Hotte with scattered records elsewhere. The species was introduced to Puerto Rico and Guadeloupe. There are no eBird records from the former island and very few from the latter. The Hispaniolan parakeet inhabits a wide variety of landscapes including lowland and montane forest, savanna, open woodlands, secondary forest, croplands, and urban centers such as Santo Domingo

relations.subject: Hispaniolan parakeet
relations.evidence: Continuing habitat loss and persecution as a crop pest are the principal threats
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
